item.title: Moldavite
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: GLASS CREATED BY METEORITE IMPACT

Structure: AMORPH

Formation: TERTIARY

Rune: ODIN

In the realm of crystals, moldavite carries the energy of transformation – a symbol of freedom, boundlessness, and the willingness to let go of all veils.

Its archetype is the magician – someone who does not remain in the familiar, but redefines the boundaries of reality and surrenders to the unknown.

Moldavite stands for letting go, trust, and self-knowledge. It brings boundless expanse and lifts all veils – not gently, but with the force of a meteorite impact.

Its origin is unique. Fifteen million years ago, a 1.5 km meteorite struck the Earth. The energy generated was so enormous that the rock that was hit compressed under extreme pressure and heat until it melted. During its flight, it cooled and turned into natural glass.

That is why it carries this unleashed energy: moldavite reminds us that true transformation cannot be planned. That sometimes something powerful must come from outside to show us who we really are. That fate shapes us – and we shape fate at the same time.

It promotes dreams and memories. It releases us from rigid attachments of all kinds. It promotes the realization that we are spiritual beings – not bodies having spiritual experiences, but spiritual beings having a human experience.

Guided by the power of destiny – the inevitable that shapes us – moldavite is here to give freedom and remind that we are safe even when we let go of control. That we ourselves are the storm of life, and that we can trust, because we have shaped our destiny – now it can shape us.

Topics:
Freedom, Boundlessness, Letting go, Trust, Connection, Wisdom, Vitality, Cheerfulness, Self-awareness, Brings boundless expansiveness and lifts all veils, Promotes dreams and memories, Releases rigid attachments of all kinds, Promotes the realization of being a spiritual being

item.title: Onyx
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: BLACK CHALCEDONY

Structure: TRIGONAL

Formation: PRIMARY / SECONDARY

Rune: THORN

In the realm of crystals, onyx carries the energy of order – a symbol of assertiveness, intellect, and the willingness to face one's own shadows.

Its archetype is the ruler – someone who does not shy away from responsibility, but accepts it and leads with a sober mind.

Onyx stands for self-confidence, self-awareness, and the ability not to lie to oneself. It improves sober thinking, logic, and powers of argumentation – not to manipulate others, but to separate truth from illusion.

Its power lies in its color: deep black light that absorbs rather than reflects. In ancient times, it was considered a protective stone against misfortune and black magic. It was given to the dead as a burial gift so that they would be protected on their journey to the afterlife.

That is why it carries this dominant energy: onyx reminds us that only purity can reflect the absolute truth. That true leadership does not come from blind power, but from the willingness to face one's own shadows and take responsibility.

It promotes a sense of responsibility and increases resilience. It sharpens the sense of hearing not only physically, but also the ability to truly listen to what is. It helps with heart, stomach, and spleen problems and improves nerve function.

Guided by the power of protection that comes not from defense but from clarity and order, onyx is here to give assertiveness – and to remind that true strength sometimes means pausing and waiting for better times instead of rushing blindly ahead.

Topics:
Assertiveness, Intellect, Shadow work, Strengthens self-confidence and self-awareness, Improves clear thinking, logic, and reasoning skills, Promotes a sense of responsibility, Sharpens the sense of hearing, Increases resilience, Helps with heart, stomach, and spleen problems, Improves nerve function

item.title: Moonstone
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: FELDSPAR

Structure: MONOCLINIC / TRICLINIC

Formation: PRIMARY

Rune: INGUZ

In the realm of crystals, moonstone carries the energy of devotion – a symbol of intuition, sensitivity, and connection to the natural rhythms of life.

Its archetype is the lover – someone who lives not through control, but through connection, openness, and a willingness to surrender to the flow of life.

Moonstone represents inspiration, mindfulness, and clairvoyance. It broadens the horizon and at the same time helps to remain realistic – a rare balance between vision and grounding.

Its power lies in its history. Since ancient times, it has been revered as a power stone that amplifies the properties of the moon on Earth. In Arab countries, it is considered a beneficial family stone that is said to bring fertility, many children, and devoted love.

That is why it carries this receptive energy: moonstone reminds us that true fertility – whether physical or spiritual – cannot be forced. That some things only come about when we open ourselves and receive, rather than directing and controlling.

It makes our emotional life easier and more accessible. It harmonizes hormone cycles and natural rhythms. It reminds us that we are part of a greater whole – and that no tree can grow to heaven until its roots reach down to hell.

Guided by the power of fertility and new beginnings, moonstone is here to provide comfort – and to remind that true growth is not linear, but cyclical, like the phases of the moon.

Topics:
Intuition, Inspiration, Mindfulness, Sensitivity, Clairvoyance, Freedom, Security, Fertility, Birth, Broadens horizons while helping to remain realistic, Makes emotional life easier and more accessible, Harmonizes hormone cycles and natural rhythms

item.title: Petrified Wood
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: FOSSIL WOOD

Structure: AMORPHOUS / TRIGONAL

Formation: SECONDARY

Rune: EOH

In the realm of crystals, petrified wood carries the energy of rootedness – a symbol of down-to-earthness, contentment, and the wisdom of change.

Its archetype is the everyman – someone who does not take off, but is at peace with himself and anchored in the here and now.

Petrified wood stands for well-being, stability, and the ability to stay focused. It makes feelings flow more freely and easier to express – not through pressure, but through inner security.

Its power lies in its transformation. Millions of years old wood that turned to stone – an organic being that has passed through time and change while retaining its structure. It reminds us that change does not have to mean loss.

That is why it carries this grounding certainty: petrified wood teaches us that true stability does not come from rigidity, but from the willingness to allow oneself to be transformed – while still remaining true to oneself.

It helps us to take advantage of external changes instead of resisting them. It allows us to see the beautiful sides of life and strengthens our bond with our fellow human beings. It gives us the ability to focus our actions on a specific goal and to see what we have started through to the end.

Guided by the wisdom of change – the eternal cycle of becoming and passing away – petrified wood is here to give a sense of groundedness and to remind that everything is connected.

Topics:
Well-being, Down-to-earthness, Contentment, Makes stable and rooted in oneself, Makes feelings flow more freely and easier to express, Helps to be fully focused on the task at hand, Allows to see the beautiful sides of life, Helps to take advantage of changes coming from outside

item.title: Aventurine
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: FUCHSITE QUARTZ

Structure: MONOCLINIC / TRIGONAL

Formation: TERTIARY

Rune: EOLH

In the realm of crystals, aventurine carries the energy of lightness – a symbol of lightheartedness, joie de vivre, and the art of letting go.

Its archetype is the fool – someone who does not need to control everything, but trusts that life will carry them.

Aventurine stands for optimism, relaxation, and inner balance. It helps with stress, nervousness, and sleep disorders – not by numbing, but by reminding us that not everything has to be difficult.

Its power lies in its name. “A ventura” means “on good luck” – and that is exactly the energy it carries within it. The ancient Greeks believed it gave courage and optimism. Not the courage of a warrior, but the courage of trust.

That is why it conveys this playful certainty: life does not always have to be serious. Sometimes the best thing we can do is let go of control and see what happens.

Aventurine strengthens inner balance and the power of self-determination. It helps to let go of worries and circular thoughts – those endless loops that keep us from the present moment. It soothes rashes, inflammation, and sunburn.

Guided by the power of protection that comes not from control but from trust, aventurine is here to give us rest – and to remind that we have nothing to fear, because we carry the entire universe within us.

Topics:
Carefree, Zest for life, Lightness, Courage, Optimism, Relaxation, Recuperation, Strengthens inner balance, Promotes self-determination, Helps with stress, nervousness, and sleep disorders, Helps to let go of worries and repetitive thoughts, Alleviates rashes, inflammation, and sunburn

item.title: Green Tourmaline
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: GREEN BORON RING SILICATE

Structure: TRIGONAL

Formation: PRIMARY

Rune: WYN

In the realm of crystals, green tourmaline carries the energy of joy – a symbol of gratitude, hope, and the ability to see the wonders of life.

Its archetype is the fool – someone who does not dwell on the seriousness of life, but walks the rocky path with their head held high, seeing beauty along the way.

Green tourmaline stands for self-realization, grounding, and self-love. It promotes reorientation and clarity – not through planning, but through flexibility and openness to what is to come.

Its power lies in its diversity. Tourmaline has been known since ancient times, but was often confused with other stones because it can have all the colors of the rainbow. Its name comes from the Sinhalese “turmali” – diverse stone. In the past, it was believed that it could glow from within.

That is why it carries this vibrant energy: green tourmaline reminds us that true joy does not come from possessions, but from a love of work. That we are only satisfied when we follow our calling. That balance in all things is the way.

It helps us to see the wonders of life. It detoxifies and promotes interest in our fellow human beings and the environment. It strengthens the nervous system, the heart, and the joints.

Guided by the power of joy, which opens up new dimensions, green tourmaline is here to give hope – and to remind that those who walk the rocky path with their heads held high and without doubt will always be where their light is needed.

Topics:
Gratitude, Joy, Hope, Peace, Self-realization, Grounding, Self-love, Reorientation, Clarity, Flexibility, Helps to see the wonders of life, Detoxifies, Promotes interest in fellow human beings and the environment, Strengthens the nervous system, the heart, and the joints

item.title: Sugilite
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: MINERAL-RICH RING SILICATE

Structure: HEXAGONAL

Formation: SECONDARY / TERTIARY

Rune: URUZ

In the realm of crystals, sugilite carries the energy of inner strength – a symbol of perseverance, self-realization, and the willingness to remain true to oneself.

Its archetype is the hero – someone who does not give in, but uncompromisingly sees projects through to completion and stands up for their convictions.

Sugilite stands for action and consistency, energy, and mental strength. It helps with fears and paranoia – not by numbing them, but by reminding you of your own power.

Its power lies in its rarity. It is one of the most valuable gemstones and has only been known since 1944. Its special feature is its strong, bright color. It was found in Africa, where the cradle of humanity is believed to be.

That is why it carries this unshakeable energy: sugilite reminds us that great power is available – but can only be realized with the right attitude. That it is sustained performance, not simple muscle power, that leads us to our goal. That we must find our inner sun.

It encourages us to remain true to ourselves. It helps to resolve conflicts. It gives us the strength to see projects through to completion without compromise and strengthens our will to achieve self-realization. It supports the grieving process.

Guided by the power that proves its strength in hard times, sugilite is here to give us perseverance – and to remind that dreams can come true: Desire. Wish. Believe. Receive.

Topics:
Action and consequence, Energy, Courage, Perseverance, Inner strength, Mental strength, Encourages you to remain true to yourself, Helps with fears, Helps resolve conflicts, Alleviates paranoia, Gives you the strength to complete projects without compromise, Strengthens the will to achieve self-realization, Supports the grieving process

item.title: Heliotrope
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: GREEN CHALCEDONY WITH RED SPOTS

Structure: TRIGONAL

Formation: PRIMARY

Rune: TYR

In the realm of crystals, heliotrope carries the energy of justice – a symbol of strength, courage, and the ability to set boundaries.

Its archetype is the hero – someone who does not give in, but fights for justice and remains invincible.

Heliotrope stands for immune protection, vitality, and balance. It helps you maintain control and set boundaries – not through secrecy, but through clear limits.

Its power lies in its name. The Greeks called it “solstice stone” – from the Greek “heliou tropai” (solstice). According to Greek mythology, it connects humans with the gods and the earth. In ancient Egypt, it was considered a powerful protective stone.

That is why it carries this warlike energy: the heliotrope reminds us that we are all warriors in the battle for awakening. That justice does not fall from the sky, but must be fought for. That protection from injustice means remaining invincible.

It promotes concentration and mindfulness. It strengthens the lymphatic system and the immune system. It helps with heart problems and flu. It alleviates the symptoms of colds and infections.

Guided by the power of justice that comes from victory, heliotrope is here to bring cheerfulness – and to remind that true warriors do not give up until the plan is complete.

Topics:
Immune protection, Strength, Courage, Vitality, Balance, Cheerfulness, Helps maintain control and set boundaries, Promotes concentration and mindfulness, Strengthens the lymphatic system and immune defenses, Helps with heart problems and flu, Alleviates the symptoms of colds and infections

item.title: Tiger’s Eye
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: YELLOW-BROWN FIBER QUARTZ

Structure: TRIGONAL

Formation: SECONDARY

Rune: SIGEL

In the realm of crystals, tiger's eye carries the energy of the sun – a symbol of vitality, optimism, and inner strength.

Its archetype is the hero – someone who does not shy away from the speed of life, but keeps their perspective even when everything is moving very quickly.

Tiger's eye stands for courage, protection, and objectivity. It frees us from stress and strain – not by escape, but through the ability to maintain distance while remaining present.

Its power lies in its origin. It is not a related mineral to the falcon's eye, but its end product – weathered falcon's eye that shows the cat's eye effect through glimmers of light, resembling the eye of a tiger. It is transformation in its purest form.

That is why it carries this radiant energy: tiger's eye reminds us that inspiration and vitality come from the sun. That true strength does not come from control, but from the ability to keep moving and still be centered.

It sharpens the senses and helps to maintain perspective when everything is moving very quickly. It regulates the adrenal glands and asthma attacks. It strengthens the bronchial tubes, promotes concentration, and balances the mood.

Guided by the power of the sun, which can banish evil, the tiger's eye is here to give vitality – and to remind that energy cannot be destroyed – it is only transformed.

Topics:
Courage, Protection, Security, Clarity, Distance, Objectivity, Vitality, Optimism, Inner strength, Frees you from stress and strain, Sharpens the senses, Helps you maintain clarity when everything is happening very quickly, Regulates the adrenal glands, Regulates asthma attacks, Strengthens the bronchial tubes, Promotes concentration, Balances your mood, and makes you calmer

item.title: Turquoise
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: BASIC COPPER ALUMINIUM PHOSPHATE

Structure: TRICLINIC

Formation: SECONDARY

Rune: RAIDO

In the realm of crystals, turquoise carries the energy of travel – a symbol of destiny, transformation, and the willingness to encounter oneself.

Its archetype is the explorer – someone who does not stand still but travels – externally and internally – and thereby transforms themselves.

Turquoise stands for initiation, intuition, and self-confidence. It is a protective stone, talisman, and amulet all in one – not because it wards off danger, but because it teaches us to recognize the causes of happiness and unhappiness.

Its power lies in its history. It was first introduced to Europe by the Crusaders. Its name comes from the French “pierre turquoise” – Turkish stone. For the Native Americans of Arizona, it is still a sacred stone today. In ancient Egypt and Greece, it was considered a protective stone that was supposed to warn of impending danger by changing color.

That is why it carries this transformative energy: turquoise reminds us that true travel is always a journey with the self. That we are transformed by striving for our own self. That mental activity and acumen arise when we are ready to travel.

It has a balancing and uplifting effect. It protects against outside influences and calms the inner self. It helps with exhaustion, hyperacidity, rheumatism, and gout. It relieves stomach problems and cramps of all kinds.

Guided by the power of the journey that leads us to ourselves, turquoise is here to bring success – and to remind that a good traveler has no fixed plans and is not intent on arriving.

Topics:
Destiny, Travel, Intuition, Initiation, Protective stone, Talisman, Amulet stone, Self-confidence, Success, Strength, Has a balancing and uplifting effect, Protects against outside influences, Calms the inner self, Enables one to recognize the causes of happiness and unhappiness, Helps with exhaustion, hyperacidity, rheumatism, and gout, Relieves stomach complaints and cramps of all kinds

item.title: Smokey Quartz
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: BROWN CRYSTAL QUARTZ

Structure: TRIGONAL

Formation: PRIMARY / TERTIARY

Rune: PEORTH

In the realm of crystals, smokey quartz carries the energy of grounding – a symbol of relaxation, realism, and the ability to cope with loss.

Its archetype is the sage – someone who does not dwell in illusions, but views things soberly and acts pragmatically.

Smoky quartz stands for vitality, courage, and coping with grief. It protects against negative energies, electrosmog, and geopathy – not by shielding, but by grounding.

Its power lies in its color. A smokey brown crystal quartz that owes its name to its color. Since ancient times, it has been considered a protective stone against danger and is said to help overcome grief. Particularly dark smokey quartz is called “Morion.”

That is why it carries this realistic energy: Smokey quartz reminds us that not everything that appears to be is what it claims to be. That we must look beyond the surface of things. That starting something always brings rewards – but that we must remain pragmatic.

It alleviates the effects of stress and has a relaxing and soothing effect. It strengthens the nerves and promotes sober, realistic, and pragmatic thinking. It opens our eyes to a new path in life and restores vitality.

Guided by the wisdom of opportunity – that secrets will be revealed and hard-earned prosperity will come – smokey quartz is here to provide grounding and to remind that the mind is a devil that claims to be me.

Topics:
Relaxation, Vitality, Courage, Coping with grief and loss, Protects against negative energies, electrosmog, and geopathy, Alleviates the effects of stress, Has a relaxing and soothing effect, Strengthens the nerves, Promotes sober, realistic, and pragmatic thinking

item.title: Malachite
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: BASIC COPPER CARBONATE

Structure: MONOCLINIC

Formation: PRIMARY / SECONDARY

Rune: ODEL

In the realm of crystals, malachite carries the energy of creativity – a symbol of adventure, intense living, and the power of imagination.

Its archetype is the creator – someone who does not passively consume, but actively creates and reinvents the world through their imagination.

Malachite stands for imagination, creativity, and a deep emotional life. It calms the mind and sharpens the inner gaze – not through withdrawal, but through immersion in one's own inner landscape.

Its power lies in its color. Its name comes from the Greek “malache” – mallow – because its color resembles the foliage of the mallow plant. It was already known to the Egyptians, Greeks, Romans, ancient Chinese, and Mayans. The Egyptians made amulets and scarabs from it and called it the “midwife stone” because it was believed to ease labor pains.

That is why it carries this creative energy: malachite reminds us that true home is not found in the external, but in the internal. That home is the inner sanctuary – the private sphere within yourself that you can shape and create.

It gives hope in dark times. It promotes imagination and determination. It helps with stomach pains of all origins, stimulates the brain, nerves, and liver, detoxifies, and helps with rheumatism and cramps.

Guided by the power of the home we create within ourselves, malachite is here to bestow creativity – and to remind that you are not what you have – but what you have is you.

Topics:
Adventure, Intense life, Imagination, Deepens emotional life, Stimulates creativity, Calms the mind and sharpens inner vision, Gives hope in dark times, Promotes imagination and determination, Helps with stomach aches of any origin, Stimulates the brain, nerves, and liver, Detoxifies, Helps with rheumatism and cramps

item.title: Tourmaline Quartz
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: TOURMALINE NEEDLES (SCHÖRL) IN QUARTZ

Structure: TRIGONAL

Formation: PRIMARY / TERTIARY

Rune: NYD

In the realm of crystals, tourmaline quartz carries the energy of harmony – a symbol of the union of polarities and inner peace.

Its archetype is the everyman – someone who does not live through extremes, but through balance, down-to-earthness, and the ability to integrate opposites.

Tourmaline quartz stands for self-awareness, steadfastness, and adaptability. It brings clarity to chaotic situations – not through simplification, but through acceptance of what is.

Its power lies in its composition. Black tourmaline (Schorl) enclosed in clear rock crystal – darkness and light united in one stone. In ancient China, it was considered a stone of harmony, bringing yin and yang into balance.

That is why it carries this balancing energy: tourmaline quartz reminds us that true harmony does not come from excluding opposites, but from integrating them. That we do not have to choose between light and dark, but can carry both within us.

It helps to resolve inner struggles and conflicts. It enables opposites to be brought into harmony. It releases tension, cramps, and hardening – both physically and mentally.

Guided by the wisdom of necessity – that dreams become reality when we are patient and take our time – tourmaline quartz is here to bring serenity and remind that our wealth is not measured by what we have, but by what we do not need.

Topics:
Connects polarities, Keeps you vital and flexible, Self-awareness, Clarity in chaotic situations, Stability, Adaptability, Helps resolve inner struggles and conflicts, Enables opposites to be brought into harmony, Relieves tension, cramps, and stiffness

item.title: Rock Crystal
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: TRANSPARENT CRYSTAL

Structure: TRIGONAL

Formation: PRIMARY / TERTIARY

Rune: MAN

In the realm of crystals, rock crystal carries the energy of transformation – a symbol of maturity, expanded consciousness, and the connection between worlds.

Its archetype is the magician – someone who does not remain in the visible realm, but bridges the boundaries between 3D reality and higher consciousness.

Rock crystal stands for union, understanding, and clarity. It improves perception and strengthens one's own point of view – not through hardness, but through crystal-clear presence.

Its power lies in its structure. It consists of about 65% silicon – the mineral that makes up the earth. Its crystal structure has the shape of a helix spiral and amplifies energies more powerfully than any other stone. It is the central stone of light.

That is why it carries this alchemical energy: rock crystal reminds us that we are not just humans having spiritual experiences – we are spiritual beings having a human experience. That we are the web and at the same time its weaver.

In ancient times, it was considered to be fossilized ice that could no longer melt. The Greeks called it “krystallos” – ice. The Romans believed it to be the seat of the gods. Buddhists used it to connect to higher powers. It has always been used to ward off black magic, curses, and spells.

Guided by the power of the self – that part of us that makes the right decisions at the right time – rock crystal is here to bring clarity – recognize the web and yourself as its weaver.

Topics:
Maturity, Experience, Expansion of consciousness, Union, Understanding, Clarity, Neutrality, Improved perception, Strengthens one's own point of view, Improves memory, Promotes understanding and presence, Releases blockages

item.title: Red Tiger’s Eye
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: FIBROUS QUARTZ

Structure: TRIGONAL

Formation: SECONDARY

Rune: KEN

In the realm of crystals, the red tiger's eye carries the energy of security – a symbol of warmth, closeness, and the power to create a home within oneself.

Its archetype is the caregiver – someone who protects not through distance, but through closeness, care, and unconditional presence.

The red tiger's eye stands for insight, inner peace, and concentration. It helps us find our way in new surroundings – not through adaptation, but through inner security that can take root anywhere.

Its power lies in its history. Even in ancient times, it was used by various peoples as a protective stone against evil. It was believed to enable dangers to be recognized early on – like a watchful eye watching over those who carry it with them.

That is why it carries this protective energy: the red tiger's eye reminds us that true security does not come from control, but from vigilance. That we can care for ourselves and others without giving ourselves up.

It sharpens the mind and promotes mindfulness. It gives warmth and strengthens friendship. It strengthens bones and joints, helps the bronchial tubes, and alleviates colds and seizures.

Guided by the power of fire, which does not burn but warms, the red tiger's eye is here to provide security – and to remind that reality is a construct of the mind and that we shape our experience through our perception.

Topics:
Clarity, Inner peace, Security, Concentration, Attention, Mindfulness, Warmth, Comfort, Sharpens the mind, Alertness, Friendship, Closeness, Helps you find your way in a new environment, Strengthens bones and joints, Helps the bronchial tubes, Alleviates colds and seizures

item.title: Lapis Lazuli
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: LASURITE ROCK

Structure: CUBIC

Formation: TERTIARY

Rune: LAGUZ

In the realm of crystals, lapis lazuli carries the energy of truth – a symbol of sincerity, dignity, and the ability to raise one's voice.

Its archetype is the sage – someone who does not live by deception, but by authenticity and a willingness to speak and accept the truth.

Lapis lazuli stands for sociability, friendship, and personal responsibility. It acts on the throat chakra – the energy center that enables us to express ourselves and be heard.

Its power lies in its history. As early as the 3rd millennium BC, it was considered a coveted commodity. The Silk Road is said to have been called the Lapis Lazuli Road. For the ancient Egyptians, it was the most precious thing they could give their pharaohs as a burial gift.

That is why it carries this clear energy: lapis lazuli reminds us that true power does not come from lies, but from the courageous willingness to speak the truth – even when it is uncomfortable.

It helps us to speak and accept the truth. It helps with throat, larynx, and vocal cord complaints—both physically and energetically.

Guided by the power of water, which tests us and brings us closer to ourselves, lapis lazuli is here to give us authenticity – and to remind that inner strength means knowing the depths of the self and swimming through them without fear.

Topics:
Truth, Sincerity, Dignity, Sociability, Friendship, Throat chakra, Personal responsibility, Authenticity, Helps to tell and accept the truth, Helps with throat, larynx, and vocal cord complaints

item.title: Moss Agate
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: CHALCEDONY WITH GREEN STREAKS

Structure: TRIGONAL

Formation: PRIMARY

Rune: JERA

In the realm of crystals, moss agate carries the energy of renewal – a symbol of liberation, lightness, and the natural cycle of life.

Its archetype is the rebel – someone who does not allow themselves to be trapped by old patterns, but has the courage to break out and start anew.

Moss agate stands for liberation from heaviness, pressure, and stress. It raises awareness and promotes communication – not through loud words, but through inner clarity.

Its power lies in its appearance. Its inclusions look like moss – like living greenery that lives on even in the stone. It reminds us that nothing really dies, but only transforms.

That is why it carries this liberating energy: moss agate helps to change boring lifestyles and break behavioral patterns such as addiction or fear. It shows that even with major problems, simple solutions can be found.

It stimulates the mind and cleanses the tissue. It helps with coughs, colds, and stubborn infections. It reduces fever and cleanses the lymphatic system and respiratory tract.

Guided by the wisdom of the harvest – the cycle of giving and taking – moss agate is here to bring lightness and to remind that the only constant in life is change.

Topics:
Liberation, Lightness, Carefreeness, Frees from heaviness, pressure, and stress, Raises awareness, Promotes communication, Stimulates the mind, Cleanses the tissue, Helps with coughs, colds, and persistent infections, Reduces fever, Cleanses the lymphatic system and respiratory tract

item.title: Peace Agate
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: WHITE AGATE

Structure: TRIGONAL

Formation: PRIMARY / SECONDARY

Rune: IS

In the realm of crystals, peace agate carries the energy of silence – a symbol of calm, peace, and the power of pause.

Its archetype is the innocent – someone who does not live through mistrust, but through trust and the willingness to feel secure.

Peace agate stands for inner composure, kindness, and lightheartedness. It promotes tolerance and forbearance – not as weakness, but as an expression of inner strength.

Its power lies in its history. Even in ancient Egypt, it was carved into scarabs – those lucky charms and protective stones that accompanied every Egyptian. In ancient Rome, it was believed that agate discs could bring power and wealth.

That is why it carries this calming energy: peace agate reminds us that sometimes the best thing we can do is to put certain things on hold. That patience is a virtue that allows us to understand things crystal clear later on.

It gives self-confidence and warmth. It alleviates aggression and brings balance. It takes away the fear of being alone and provides comfort in any form of separation.

Guided by the power of preservation and cooling, peace agate is here to provide security – and to remind that energy always follows intention.

Topics:
Calm, Peace, Security, Strengthens inner composure for kindness and lightheartedness, Promotes tolerance and forbearance, Gives self-confidence, Warmth, Alleviates aggression, Balance, Takes away the fear of being alone, Provides comfort in any form of separation

item.title: Labradorite
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: COLORFUL IRIDESCENT FELDSPAR

Structure: TRICLINIC

Formation: PRIMARY

Rune: HAGALAZ

In the realm of crystals, labradorite carries the energy of liberation – a symbol of reflection, truth, and the ability to see through illusions.

Its archetype is the sage – someone who does not remain on the surface, but looks deeper and recognizes what is real.

Labradorite stands for intuition, sensitivity, and psychic gifts. It strengthens emotional depth and brings forgotten memories to the surface – not to dwell on the past, but to understand.

Its power lies in its appearance. Its iridescent play of colors – known as labradorescence – reminds us that nothing has just one facet. That the truth changes depending on the angle from which we view it.

That is why it carries this clarifying energy: labradorite helps us to recognize illusions and let go of old patterns. It frees us from limitations that prevent us from seeing what is.

It stimulates imagination, creativity, and enthusiasm. It promotes alertness and powers of observation. It broadens our horizons while helping us to remain realistic – a rare balance between vision and grounding.

Guided by the wisdom that those who look outward dream, but those who look inward awaken, labradorite is here to offer protection – and to remind that we can be content with what we have in order to create a stress-free environment.

Topics:
Reflection, Truth, Protection, Strengthens intuition, emotional depth, and psychic gifts, Brings forgotten memories to light, Sensitivity, Helps recognize illusions, Promotes appreciation, Stimulates imagination, creativity, and enthusiasm, Promotes alertness and powers of observation

item.title: Rose Quartz
item.dc:creator: Dada Chi
item.content:encoded:
Stone: PINK QUARTZ

Structure: TRIGONAL

Formation: PRIMARY

Rune: GYFU

In the realm of crystals, rose quartz carries the energy of devotion – a symbol of love, compassion, and the art of giving.

Its archetype is the lover – someone who lives not through control, but through connection, openness, and a willingness to open their heart.

Rose quartz stands for charity, sensitivity, and empathy. It brings open-mindedness and promotes romance – not as naive infatuation, but as a deep willingness to reveal oneself and share.

Its power lies in its origin. According to Greek and Roman mythology, it was brought to Earth by Eros, the god of love, to give love to humans. It has always been considered a stone of fertility and charity.

That is why it carries this opening energy: rose quartz reminds us that true love does not want to possess, but to share. That generosity and giving happen in the light of love.

In cases of heartbreak, it prevents the heart from closing – it helps with letting go and alleviates suffering. Rose quartz supports harmonious coexistence and strengthens helpfulness. It refines perception and clarifies both one's own needs and the wishes of others.

Guided by the power of love that does not take but gives, rose quartz is here to bestow compassion – and to remind that the universe is eternal because it does not animate itself but gives life to all things as it transforms.

Topics:
Compassion, Love, Charity, Sensitivity, Empathy, Brings open-mindedness, Promotes romance, Supports harmonious coexistence, Strengthens helpfulness, Refines perception, Clarifies one's own needs and the desires of others
